The mission line ('craft deeply human stories through innovative technology... helping creative production teams build expertise in evolving tools') paraphrases LAIKA's mission without claiming Jamie's animation experience — honest.

checks.check: MINOR — NextGen bullet 4 '5 cross-functional projects' number
checks.evidence: Tailored bullet: 'Improved employee experience via 5 cross-functional projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D.' Canonical bullet (content_library): 'Elevated employee experience through projects focused on engagement, rewards & recognition, survey development, and L&D.' The quantifier '5' is NOT in the canonical source — it is a specificity ADDED during tailoring. Low-severity (the canonical says 'projects' plural and lists 4 named domains, so 5 is plausible and not a metric Jamie would be challenged on), but it is technically an unsourced number. NOTE for the builder: prefer the canonical un-numbered phrasing on future renders, or confirm the count with Jamie. Not a blocking fabrication.

notes: 9 evidence-bearing checks, 8 clean PASS + 1 PASS-with-note (NextGen '5 projects' unsourced quantifier — non-blocking). The headline risk for this STRETCH — inventing technical-systems/animation/agile/pipeline depth to chase the JD — did NOT materialize anywhere. The package is disciplined: it leads with Jamie's genuine training-delivery/documentation/needs-assessment work, keeps ODN truthful and unblended, uses only canonical skills (no Jira/agile/bug-tracking), no banned numbers, and the cover holds the locked skeleton without free-writing. Cover mission line paraphrases LAIKA without claiming animation work. Truthful sponsorship handling deferred to form (job.json flags sponsorship UNKNOWN at LAIKA — Jamie should verify on apply). PASS clears the package for submit on quality grounds.
fit_take: Honest fit is LOW-MODERATE and this is a genuine STRETCH. The JD's REQUIRED quals (agile software dev terms/tools, software lifecycle/engineering terminology) and PREFERRED quals (feature animation/VFX production) are real domains Jamie lacks — she cannot credibly speak to story-mapping-for-developers, pipeline workflows, or bug-tracking-to-engineers in an interview. Her genuine overlap is real but partial: training delivery at scale, guide/tutorial/release-note creation, end-user needs surveys, documentation, MS Office + Adobe InDesign. That covers roughly half the JD's job-functions and almost none of the technical-engineering-adjacent ones. With 200+ applicants and a tech-support/animation-studio screen likely filtering on agile + production-tool fluency, conversion odds are low. Geography is the one strong plus (Hillsboro = Portland-metro, in-bounds) and LAIKA is a marquee local employer. RECOMMENDATION: STAGE, do not auto-submit as a top-priority. It's a defensible low-cost apply IF the run has spare capacity and the build is already done (it is, and it's clean) — submitting a truthful, well-built package costs little. But it should NOT displace at-level P1/P1b/P1c fits in the queue, and outreach energy is better spent elsewhere. If David wants volume + a local-employer shot, submit it; if he wants fit-quality focus per the YOE/fit-dig directive, drop it to the bottom of the stack or skip. Quality gate = PASS; fit gate = low-priority STRETCH.
